query: Emmanuel Macron condition opening French embassy Palestinian state recognition UN General Assembly

mako.raw:
{"query":"Emmanuel Macron condition opening French embassy Palestinian state recognition UN General Assembly","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1675,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/ckg5g4p3245o","title":"France to recognise Palestinian state in September","content":"In a post on X, Macron said the formal announcement would be made at a session of the UN General Assembly in New York.
The US "strongly rejects" Macron's announcement, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said, calling the decision "reckless".
"Finally, we must build the State of Palestine, ensure its viability, and ensure that by accepting its demilitarisation and fully recognising Israel, it contributes to the security of all in the Middle East.
Currently, the State of Palestine is recognised by more than 140 of the 193 member states of the UN.
A few European Union countries, including Spain and Ireland, are among them.
On Friday, a majority of MPs on the foreign affairs committee, which scrutinises UK foreign policy, called for the immediate recognition of the state of Palestine, adding it should make the move "while there is still a state to recognise"."},{"url":"https://www.lemonde.fr/en/international/article/2025/09/22/macron-recognizes-state-of-palestine-for-peace-vows-to-keep-up-existential-fight-against-antisemitism_6745641_4.html","title":"France recognizes State of Palestine, Macron declares at UN","content":"French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, September 22, recognized a Palestinian state, leading a UN summit that already spurred other Western governments to take the landmark step that has infuriated Israel.
The United States, Israel's crucial diplomatic and military supporter, had unsuccessfully urged its allies to drop the recognition plans, with President Donald Trump saying that a state can only come through negotiations.
Germany, while more critical than Washington of Israel's actions in Gaza, also broke with France and Britain and will not recognize a Palestinian state.
Israel said it would skip an emergency UN Security Council meeting on Gaza Tuesday because of the Jewish New Year, calling the timing "regrettable.""},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/world/2025/jul/24/france-to-recognise-palestinian-state-at-un-general-assembly-macron-says","title":"France to recognise Palestinian state at UN general assembly, Macron says | France | The Guardian","content":"France will recognise a Palestinian state in September at the UN general assembly, Emmanuel Macron has said.
Macron published a letter sent to the Palestinian Authority president, Mahmoud Abbas, confirming France’s intention to become the first major western power to recognise a Palestinian state.
French officials initially weighed up the move in advance of a UN conference, which France and Saudi Arabia had planned to co-host in June, to lay out the parameters for a route to a Palestinian state while ensuring Israel’s security.
Britain’s prime minister Keir Starmer announced he would hold a call with counterparts in Germany and France on efforts to stop the fighting on Friday, adding that a ceasefire would “put us on a path to the recognition of a Palestinian state”.
Norway, Spain, Ireland and Slovenia all announced recognition in 2024 as Israel’s military campaign in Gaza continued."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/7/24/macron-says-france-will-recognise-palestinian-state-in-september","title":"Macron says France will recognise Palestinian state in September | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera","content":"“I will solemnly announce this at the United Nations General Assembly in September this year,” he added.
In his post on Thursday, Macron shared a letter to Palestinian Authority (PA) President Mahmoud Abbas in which he outlined his intent.
“A Palestinian state in these conditions would be a launchpad to annihilate Israel,” the statement read."}]}

{"query":"Emmanuel Macron condition opening French embassy Palestinian state recognition UN General Assembly","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zgAcqNQAT_k","title":"Macron says hostage release is a "requirement" before embassy opens in a Palestinian state","content":"Macron says hostage release is a "requirement" before embassy opens in a Palestinian state
Face the Nation
807000 subscribers
382 likes
38879 views
21 Sep 2025
French President Emmanuel Macron tells "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that he plans this week at the U.N. General Assembly to formalize his country's decision to recognize a Palestinian state. But, he added that it is a "requirement" that Hamas release the remaining hostages before France would open an embassy.

"Face the Nation" is America's premier Sunday morning public affairs program. The broadcast is one of the longest-running news programs in the history of television, having debuted Nov. 7, 1954, on CBS. Every Sunday, "Face the Nation" moderator and CBS News senior foreign affairs correspondent Margaret Brennan welcomes leaders, newsmakers, and experts to a lively round table discussion of current events and the latest news.

And in the name of our friendship with the Palestinian people, for whom we want the United Nations’ initial promise, that of two States living side by side in peace and security, to become a reality.","score":0.6002124,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/france-spearheads-efforts-to-recognize-palestinian-state","title":"France spearheads efforts to recognize Palestinian state","content":"He also said France would not open an embassy in Palestine until Hamas releases Israelis taken hostage during its attack on Oct. 7, 2023.","score":0.59796065,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/9/22/france-recognises-palestinian-state-ahead-of-the-unga-meeting","title":"France among six more countries to formally recognise Palestinian statehood | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera","content":"French President Emmanuel Macron addresses delegates during a high-level meeting of heads of state on a two-state solution between Israel and the Palestinians at United Nations headquarters in New York City, U.S. The leaders of six countries, including France, have moved to recognise Palestinian statehood at a high-level summit ahead of the annual United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) meeting in New York. Alongside France, which co-convened the meeting with Saudi Arabia on Monday in New York, Andorra, Belgium, Luxembourg, Malta and Monaco said they were recognising a Palestinian state. “It falls on us, this responsibility, to do everything in our power to preserve the possibility of a two-state solution,” Macron said.
